candidates.stmt:
- Hold regular surgeries to address community concerns - Implement cost-cutting measures by reducing non-essential spending - Take action to stop anti-social behaviour in the burn valley ward

"I was born and raised in the Burn Valley ward. Over the past 15 years, I have been dedicated to serving our residents through my work at Yodel, walking the streets 6 days a week all year round. This experience has given me a deep understanding of the challenges and issues faced by our community. I am committed to being a strong advocate for all residents at the council."

My name is India Robertson, and I am standing as the Green Party candidate for Burn Valley in the upcoming elections in Hartlepool on the 7th of May. 
I have lived in Hartlepool for 5 years and feel as though, throughout that time, I have learned how warm, friendly and hard-working the people in this town are. By running for a local council position, I hope to give back to the community that has been so welcoming and supportive of me over the years.

As much as I love this community, I believe that there are major issues that need addressing, for example:
-Renters’ rights are being dismissed 
-Public transport is inaccessible to many
-Our most vulnerable communities’ needs are being neglected 
-Many local businesses are struggling to make ends meet
But most of all, people are feeling hopeless and like their voices don’t matter. I vow to change that.

If I am elected, the views of the residents of Burn Valley will be my priority, and I will communicate with them regularly to ensure their voices are heard and their needs are prioritised.

If anything that I have said in this statement resonates with you, I would appreciate your support. Please don’t hesitate to get in touch if you have any questions or would like to discuss anything further. With your help, I truly believe that I can change Burn Valley for the better!

A mum of four, Rachel worked in housing for 25 years, primarily focused on supporting those leaving care.  Now a caseworker she advocates for people with a whole range issues. Chair of Governors of a local primary school,  she has taken an active role in supporting children.

As a councillor she has supported her community around parking issues, road and beach safety and challenged on a number of issues including at the highest level on the crustaceans die off as well as on housing for families with a disabled family member and safety for shop workers.

Rachel is a highly visible and accessible councillor who is keen to continue to support her ward.

https://www.facebook.com/share/1MrFAb6kY6/

I have worked as a mental health social worker, community development worker and a detached youth worker.  I presently jointly run a service for homeless people that provides over seventy people with a home that’s affordable and work with them to have greater choices and independence to move positively forward in their lives and to have the belief that tomorrow will be a better day.  We are extremely proud of this achievement as we started 8 years ago with only two people.

If elected I would like to work close with the community to
Reduce child and family poverty and empower local communities. 
Reduce high rents and make more affordable. 
Make homes more affordable and reduce homelessness.
Reduce fear of crime, anti social behaviour and work in partnership with communities and agencies. 
Reduce fly tipping and engage multi-agency services to reduce impact on the environment.
Create clean spaces for everyone especially for children to play 
Engage a range of young people into positive activities 
Reduce cost of living, high energy bills, 
Food prices, 
Bring services back into public ownership and make transport more affordable.
introduce basic income. 
Improve access to dental care, GP appointments.
Improve mental health services and work with GP services, Mental health teams and patients to ensure a more responsive and joined up service.

Hi everyone, I’m Sara Hamouda, and I’m proud to be standing to represent Rural West ward in this year’s local elections.

I believe residents deserve professional, accountable representation, councillors who show up, listen, and take their responsibilities seriously.

Rural West’s councillors were elected as Conservatives, then switched parties or went independent without consulting residents. Many have also been absent from parish meetings and community life. Our communities deserve better than that.

I bring over 18 years’ experience in education and community support, working closely with families, schools, local organisations, and through my work across the church supporting people and bringing communities together. Those experiences have shown me how much can be achieved when someone is visible, approachable, and prepared to put the work in.

My promise is simple: I will be present, I will listen, and I will be the active, engaged councillor that West Park, Naisberry Park, Elwick, Dalton Piercy, and Wynyard need.

Over the coming months, I’ll be out across the ward speaking with residents. If there’s something you’d like to raise, whether it’s a concern, an issue on your street, or an idea to improve our area, please get in touch. I’m always happy to listen.

My Credentials:
✔ Proud disabled local resident
Lived experience of accessibility barriers, local services, and everyday challenges facing residents.

✔ Over 18 years’ experience in education & community support
Long-term professional background helping people, solving problems, and working with families.

✔ School Governor
Experience of accountability, decision-making, safeguarding, and supporting children and parents.

✔ Active involvement in local church-based community services
Practical community work, visibility, and support for residents.

✔ Member of the Hartlepool Conservation Area Advisory Committee
Direct involvement in local planning, heritage, and neighbourhood decision-making.

✔ Mum of two
P

As a young working-class local, I’ve experienced the ups and downs of the area over time. I’m tired of being disappointed. I’m ready for real positive change for Victoria Ward and this town. Are you? If elected I will focus on putting the people first, creating a brighter future and making a safer community to live in. To put the heart back into Hartlepool.

To be more precise, my policies are:

- Investing in making streets cleaner and communities safer. Reducing environment waste and pollution to protect our people, spaces & wildlife.

- Ensuring accessible and affordable housing where landlords and housing companies can’t abuse the right to profit. Using rent freezes for social housing tenants as a start.

- Investing into infrastructure, communities and small business to grow the heart of the town.

- Uplifting art and culture in the town to enrich its history while providing jobs, opportunity and tourism.

- Improving places of education and youth programmes to ensure a better future for young people. Helping to develop innovation and in turn reducing potential crime related offences.

- Developing the health sector and supporting NHS practices, including mental health programmes.

- Tackling addiction through rehabilitation treatment instead of criminalisation. Utilising the new drug and alcohol treatment centre to help addiction and recovery using drug regulation. In turn reducing crime rate.

- Supporting marginalised groups such as LGBTQ+ and ethnic minorities.
